title: Me has a go
wikitext:
[]: Me has a go...             Quote Post by Dartz » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:43 pm I dont do psychobabble, but I caught something on Wikipedia that started me thinking about Shinji. I dont normally do stuff like this, but when I read the article, something clicked and gears started turning. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introjection One example often used is when a child envelops representational images of his absent parents into himself, simultaneously fusing them with his own personality.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Aug 21, 2008 2:37 pm Very smart Dartz. I like where you are going. I do see that in Shinji. This is an interesting revelation, though I hope it hasn't been brought up in other threads.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Aug 21, 2008 3:09 pm float like a butterfly sting like a bee...more or less but I do agree with what you said.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Aug 22, 2008 9:20 am It does make sense... but if my memory serves me correctly, this is not the only time it was mentioned, albeit with proper psychological terms included. Interesting find...

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri Aug 22, 2008 2:48 pm Stuff like this has a tendancy to be mentioned before. Anything you can say will have been said previously and debunked in much more official terms than you can ever think off... Oh well... I just thought it was a cool thought is all.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 27, 2008 7:06 am I both like and support this theory and thread. Nice find.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Aug 27, 2008 10:47 am Good points.

Article: Me has a go
[]: Me has a go...             Quote Post by Dartz » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:43 pm I dont do psychobabble, but I caught something on Wikipedia that started me thinking about Shinji. I dont normally do stuff like this, but when I read the article, something clicked and gears started turning. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Introjection One example often used is when a child envelops representational images of his absent parents into himself, simultaneously fusing them with his own personality.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Aug 21, 2008 2:37 pm Very smart Dartz. I like where you are going. I do see that in Shinji. This is an interesting revelation, though I hope it hasn't been brought up in other threads.
